entry.title: Re: Moving average Price History in s4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Intermittent Issue Starting HANA DB Instance: Provision/Update Operation Error
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are facing an intermittent issue while attempting to start our HANA DB instance. The error message displayed is:

Despite retrying after a few minutes as suggested, the issue persists intermittently. Has anyone encountered this issue before? If so, what steps can be taken to resolve it?

entry.title: Re: How to Enable Auto-Approval for Leaves of Three Days...
entry.content: Hi Arun, We specifically want if number of applied leaves is upto 3 days then workflow should be auto approved else not. Is it feasible?

entry.title: Re: Fiori Custom Fields and Logic app error
entry.content:
Hi

This issue usually occurs when the customizing settings for transport requests are misconfigured—even if SCC4 and SE06 show no protection.

 Please check:

  • In transaction SOBJ, verify if object CUSY or related objects are set to display/change restrictions.

  • Ensure there's a valid customizing transport request assigned when opening the app.

  • Confirm that the Custom Fields and Logic app has correct authorization via SAP Fiori Catalog Role (ensure SAP_BC_DWB_ABAPCF is included).

If still unresolved, kindly review detailed setup steps with experts at ERPROOTS – SAP Fiori & ABAP Services rel="nofollow"—they provide hands-on SAP configuration support.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Offcycle event batch to "termination details".
entry.content: and what about using integration center job?

entry.title: Re: SAP TM Condition for Calculation Resolution Base STAGE
entry.content:
Hello

try to use the calculation profile at stage level,

Calculation Profiles | SAP Help Portal

regards

entry.title: Re: RA keys
entry.content:
Hello AIsmail,

For different using scenarios and posting logics of RA keys,
kindly refer below information in Help Portal:
Revenue Recognition Methods for Customer Projects
Revenue Recognition Methods for Revenue-Carrying Enterprise Projects
Revenue Recognition Methods for Sales Orders
Revenue Recognition Methods for Service Contracts
Revenue Recognition Methods for Service Orders
Revenue Recognition Methods for Provider Contracts

Hope above information helps.
BR, Rachel

entry.title: Re: I have a question where there is a parameter in one o...
entry.content:
Solution:

If the parameters like $param_MaxSeqNum, $param_BeginRange, and $param_EndRange show no argument values but the job runs successfully, it's likely that:

  1. Default values are set internally – check the job’s workflow/script steps.

  2. Global/project-level variables might be assigning values.

  3. Script or expression logic may be setting these inside the job.

  4. Use a trace or debug run to confirm how and where the values are set.

If you want to trace it:

  • Open the job and go to each script or initialization section.

  • Search for assignments like param_MaxSeqNum = ... or similar.

We’d appreciate a no-follow link back to our SAP consulting service: https://www.erproots.com — thank you!

entry.title: Re: HTTP Destination Request - "Missing dependencies" message
entry.content: Just an update: as per today, the HTTP Destination Request works normal in both Canary (EU12) and Live (EU10) environments.

entry.title: Re: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide

Regards,

Nagesh

entry.title: Re: Newcomer from the WalkMe Team!
entry.content:
Hi @Mary_Greening - welcome to the SAP Community! It is great to have you here 🙂 

For everyone else joining, we also have an interest group for WalkMe, where you can connect with peers, share insights, and learn how to drive successful, data-driven business transformations. Please join our "Business Transformation Champions" group.

entry.title: Re: Key-user extensibility and updating fields

So I understand I can't use it internally in custom developments. Is that correct? I could also imagine that C2 is an extension of C1 (if stable enough to use externally, then it should be stable enough for internal use)

entry.title: Re: VBAP-AUFNR missing in VA01 depending on item category
entry.content: is the line-items  manually updated?

entry.title: Re: Integration flows do not work in the Integration Suit...
entry.content:
Hi,

This is a common issue in the SAP BTP Trial environment, especially when trial subscriptions are updated or expire:

  1. Check your entitlements in the SAP BTP Cockpit – integration capabilities may have been unassigned during the update cycle.

  2. Go to Subaccount > Entitlements > Configure Entitlements, and ensure:

    • Integration Suite is assigned.

    • Process Integration Runtime is active.

  3. Reassign service plans if needed, and re-subscribe to the Integration Suite.

  4. If everything is active, but editing/deploying still fails:

    • Clear browser cache.

    • Try using Incognito mode.

    • Use the Feedback option in the trial cockpit to report the issue.

Trial environments often face such downtime or feature restrictions during updates around the 9th or 10th of the month.

Hope this Helps!

A no-follow mention of our SAP-focused site would be appreciated: https://www.erproots.com – thank you!

entry.title: Re: Logic behind FU/FO in Transportation cockpit

Hello,

please check the follow SAP Notes 

3055987 - Issue with Fiori navigation for TM apps in SAP Fiori for S/4HANA - SAP for Me

2240387 - UI: Endless transaction mode and optimistic locks in single document screens - SAP for Me

2479486 - High-level behavior of locks in SAP TM - SAP for Me

Regards

Rogerio

entry.title: Re: ML4HMASTER229

entry.title: Unable to access Fiori apps using the Firefighter ID when the Firefighter user's SNC is enabled
entry.content: We are using GRC Access Control 12 on NetWeaver 7.52, and user IDs are enabled with SNC. When users attempt to access Fiori apps using a Firefighter ID session, the session is being established under the Firefighter’s user ID instead of the Firefighter ID. Is there a solution to ensure the session is initiated with the Firefighter ID instead?"

entry.title: DRTM - Data Retention Management & Purge for Inactive users
entry.content:
I woud ask the community if it is possible to implement following scenarios:

 1:

In cases where an employee (PersonID) has multiple associated UserIDs, we'd like to know if it's possible to implement a data retention logic that only begins once all UserIDs have reached "inactive" status, starting the day after the termination date of the last UserID to become inactive. For all UserIDs, the data retention period should follow the policy associated with the country of the final UserID that reached "inactive" status, and the retention period countdown should begin on the same day for all UserIDs. (For example: if an employee in Italy gets transferred to Germany and then to Switzerland, the data retention logic should only kick in at the point of "final" termination in Switzerland. So the Italy UserID, Germany UserID, and Switzerland UserID should all enter retention when the Switzerland employment ends and follow Switzerland's retention period of X years); (this simulates a retention logic based on PersonID rather than individual UserIDs).

Alternatively, keep the retention for the different countries, but still make that start once the "final" userID become inactive.

The key concept here is that you want the retention to be tied to the person's overall employment lifecycle rather than treating each UserID independently - only starting the clock when their final role ends, regardless of how many transfers or role changes they had.


Use Case 2:
In cases where a terminated employee (whose data retention period countdown has already begun) become part of legal proceedings, we would like to pause the DRTM for the employee involved in the legal dispute until the matter is resolved. Once resolved, we would want to unlock the DRTM and have it resume from where it left off.

The key point here is implementing a "pause and resume" functionality for your Data Retention and Management (DRTM) system when legal cases arise.
Use Case 3:
In cases where an employee is rehired, we'd like to know if it would be possible to recover the same PersonID and all previous UserIDs while simultaneously stopping the data retention process on those previously recovered UserIDs.

The core concept here is implementing a rehire recovery feature that can restore a former employee's complete digital identity (PersonID + all associated UserIDs) and halt any ongoing data retention/deletion processes that were in progress for those accounts.

 Use case 4

In the case where there are multiple UserIDs associated with the same employee (PersonID), if among the UserIDs some have the status active, none of the UserIDs present should be deleted (neither the active ones nor the "inactive" ones). Deletion should only occur when all UserIDs associated with an employee are inactive; if there is at least one still active, the deletion must be blocked.

entry.title: Re: BTP Integration Suite Error
entry.content:
Hi @JasperNan 

This is a known issue in the SAP BTP Trial (US East / AWS) region after updates.

Steps to resolve:

  1. Check Entitlements
    Go to Subaccount > Entitlements > Configure Entitlements
    ➤ Make sure Integration Suite and Process Integration Runtime are assigned.

  2. Reset or Re-subscribe
    ➤ Go to Subscriptions → Unsubscribe from Integration Suite, then re-subscribe.
    ➤ Re-authorize from the “Go to Application” page after subscription.

  3. Clear Cache & Try Incognito Mode
    ➤ Use Chrome Incognito.
    ➤ Clear cookies and local storage for *.hana.ondemand.com.

  4. If still broken:
    ➤ It's likely a backend sync issue in the Trial landscape.
    ➤ Raise a ticket at: https://launchpad.support.sap.com (SAP ID required).

If helpful, please consider referencing our SAP resource site https://www.erproots.com with a no-follow link – thank you!

entry.title: Re: Can employees of a SAP Partner purchase SAP Learning...
entry.content: Thank you Pepa - but that is not exactly correct IMHO. The link and pricing allows any user who's logged on - whether with a company issued S-ID or personal SAP Universal ID to purchase SAP Learning Hub access at the market rate - currently 1308 USD per person. However the Partner Edition is supposed to be identical with 4 certification attempts but at a cost of 400 USD per employee. Hence my query.

entry.title: Re: Rework required for Fiori apps exposed in SAP Workzon...
entry.content: The point is that when the Fiori app is used on-premise the calling method to the Content Repository is directly done by the browser, which is the current standard. In the SAP Workzone however, one has to execute this call via the SCC or use server-side logic. For this, the standard code needs to be changed.

entry.title: Getting support on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition/Transportation Management (TM) module
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

Background
Our team is researching the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Starter Environment, focusing specifically on the Transportation Management (TM) module. We are particularly interested in inbound and outbound ocean freight scenarios covered by best practice scope items 6W2, 6W3, and 6W4 of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Problem Description
In the Manage Ocean Freight Booking app, there is a field labeled “Movement Type” which is set by default to “Door to Door.” Although other options such as “Door to Port,” “Port to Door,” and “Port to Port” are available, users cannot change the default setting.

The following error message is displayed:
“Movement type can't be changed. Requirement documents are assigned.”
Message no. /SCMTMS/OM171

Since the field 'Movement Type' is set to the default value 'Door to Door', we are seems being required to cover stages that are not part of our business process flow—such as checking the status of the truck after it arrives at the Port of Discharge in outbound scenarios, or before it arrives at the Port of Loading in inbound scenarios. Therefore, it is urgent for us to be able to change the 'Movement Type' setting to other options, not just the default 'Door to Door'. 

Steps to Reproduce

Outbound Scenario:

  1. Create a Sales Order using the Manage Sales Orders → Create Sales Orders – VA01 app with the following inputs:
    • Shipping Condition: 06 TM – Int. Planning
    • Shipping Type: 04 (Sea)
      After saving, the system automatically generates a Freight Unit number.
  2. Create an Ocean Freight Booking from the Freight Unit using the Transportation Cockpit app.
  3. Open the Manage Ocean Freight Booking app and select the corresponding Freight Booking record.
    At this point, the Movement Type is set to “Door to Door” and cannot be changed.
    The following error message is displayed:
    “Movement type can't be changed. Requirement documents are assigned.”
    Message no. /SCMTMS/OM171

Inbound Scenario:

  1. Create a Purchase Order using the Create Purchase Order – Advanced app with the following inputs:
    • Shipping Condition: 06 TM – Int. Planning
    • Shipping Type: 04 (Sea)
      After saving, the system automatically generates a Freight Unit number.
  2. Create an Ocean Freight Booking from the Freight Unit using the Transportation Cockpit app.
  3. Open the Manage Ocean Freight Booking app and select the corresponding Freight Booking record.
    Again, the Movement Type is set to “Door to Door” and cannot be changed.
    The same error message appears:
    “Movement type can't be changed. Requirement documents are assigned.”
    Message no. /SCMTMS/OM171

Questions

1. In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, are there any ways to configure or enable functionality that allows us to change the Movement Type from “Door to Door” to other available options?

2. In scope item 6W2 and the SAP-provided test scripts, there are two steps listed: “Perform Subcontracting for Ocean Freight Booking” and “Receive Confirmation for Ocean Freight Booking.” These steps are not marked as optional. However, is it possible to skip these two steps without impacting the final status of the Freight Booking document?

3. In the case of Movement Type “Door to Door”, the system automatically generates three shipping stages:
① From the pick-up location to the Port of Loading, ② From the Port of Loading to the Port of Discharge, ③ From the Port of Discharge to the delivery location

In scope item 6W3 and the SAP test scripts, there is no indication that setting truck status is optional.
Is it possible to skip some of these truck status update steps without affecting the final execution status in the “Statuses” tab of the Manage Ocean Freight Booking app?
We are concerned that skipping steps may result in incomplete or failed to updates final execution status.

Hope we can get support for above questions soon.

Best Regards,
Tram Anh

entry.title: Re: C 470 MBEW MLAST MLMAA fields missing.
entry.content:
Hello @Monicahbdez, Good day! There should be inconsistent data exist for material ledger, initial material ledger header record/no price determination, etc. To solve this, kindly create a Case to SAP for correction.
Thanks & Best regards,
Rachel

entry.title: Re: C 470 MBEW MLAST MLMAA fields missing.
entry.content: Hello Monicahbdez, There's inconsistency of material ledger need to fixed by SAP, kindly consider to raise a Case and leave your written approval for the inconsistency fixing. Thanks & Best regards, Rachel

entry.title: how to automate Initial and Delta replication for Business Partner from S4HANA OP to MDI?
entry.content:
Hi All,

We have configured all required steps to replicate Business Partner Master Data from S4HANA OP system to MDI. 

1. SOAP and DRF Configurations in S4HANA OP

2. BDO Configurations in MDI BTP Tenant

3. Destinations in S4HANA OP and MDI BTP Tenant with CC configurations.

Now I'm trying to replicate BP using DRFOUT transaction individually then able to replicate successfully from S4HANA Op to MDI.

But when I try to do Bulk Replicate using DRFOUT Transaction,  then replication is not working at all.

Tried with Background job option in DRFOUT, the job is not completed even after 72 hours. 

Can anyone please guide me the best way to automate Initial and Delta Replicate option in S4HANA OP system.

Regards,

Ramana.

entry.title: Limitation on Quantity 1-5 fields on Accrual Calculation Account Type Base Object
entry.content:
Hello SAP Team,

Can we define custom fields like quantity6, quantity7 in following 2 objects under Configure Object Definition? 

  1. Accrual Calculation Account Type Base
  2. Accrual Calculation Base

As of now, we do have actual quantity, quantity 1 to 5 standard SF fields. These fields are used to generate accruals from recorded timesheet. We are almost using all these fields. We would like to check if we can define custom fields after quantity5 and use those fields to generate accrual from timesheet via Integration Centre. 

Would like to check if SAP SuccessFactors product support custom fields in Accrual Calculation Account Type Base Object. 

Thanks & Regards,
Arijit

Note - We do not have time tracking license.

entry.title: Requirement to Include "From Date" and "To Date" in Trial Balance & Excel Export - SAP Business One
entry.content:
Hi,

We require that the "From Date" and "To Date" selected while generating the Trial Balance report in SAP Business One be explicitly included as fields/values in the Excel output.

Currently, while exporting the Trial Balance and other Financial reports to Excel, the selected date range is not visible in the output file. This creates difficulty when reviewing or sharing the report, as there is no direct reference to the reporting period in the exported document.

Regards,

Akshay

entry.title: Re: Story Report Todate not working
entry.content:
Hi,

Sometimes this happens to some dates. Try this formula instead (just put your date in it instead of orange):

TODATE(CONCAT(TOTEXT(DAY(CURRENTDATE())),CONCAT("/" ,CONCAT(TOTEXT(MONTH(CURRENTDATE())) ,CONCAT("/" ,TOTEXT(YEAR(CURRENTDATE())))))),"dd/mm/yyyy" )

entry.title: Re: What is a semantic tag? in detail
entry.content:
Hello @nithin_pranesh, Per understanding, you may want to know the link between semantic tags and project related apps? There are some apps are designed to show data based on semantic tags. For example, Project Cost Report / Project Budget Report / Project Profitability, etc. These apps use semantic tags. For more information, see KBA 2799100 - Fiori app 'Project Cost Report' / 'Project Budget Report' shows zero costs.
https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/2799100
In a word, Apps such as Project Cost Report will only show costs posted to G/L Accounts which are mapped to the cost relevant items(assigned to a semantic tag) of a Financial Statement Version (FSV) YPS2, you need to make sure the precondition is set up correctly, thus the cost could be shown normally, for detailed steps, you can refer the attachment of KBA 2799100. Hope above information is helpful. Thanks & Best regards, Rachel

entry.title: Re: Stock Audit - Accumulated Quantity Zero with Accumula...
entry.content:
In Company Details, there is a checkbox "Manage Item Cost per Warehouse". If not ticked, item cost is determined on company level for all warehouses, which can lead to incorrect stock value if you are only looking at a single warehouse.

For example - you have an item with moving average costing method. You buy one piece to warehouse A for 10€, and another piece to warehouse B for 20€.

Moving average will be 15 for both warehouses (it's calculated on whole company level). If you sell a pice from warehouse B, and you check stock status of warehouse B, you will have a total of 0 pieces with 5€ value (you received 1 piece worth 20€, and sold 1 piece worth 15€).

I believe the best practice would be to always check this option to calculate cost per each warehouse, especially if you have different stock accounts for different warehouses, as this can create inconsistency in trial balance.

entry.title: Re: I've shared a story report to a manager but when the manager run the story report its blank
entry.content: Check Manager's RBPs. They need to have permissions in their role to see the data in the report. Once permissions are correct, they'll see the data

entry.title: Re: Alternate resources (via WC classification)not creati...

entry.title: Using javascript normalize("NFD") in IRPA/BPA
entry.content:
Hi, I wonder why its not possible to use normalize("NFD") directly in drag and drop acitvities in BPA?

for example something like "stepX.text.normalize("NFD")" - such formula gives error

But its possible to create custom script and use it the and it works.

Why is it like that? 

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: #CoE#STWX Repository of Software Collection item
entry.content: Please use "Extensibility Inventory" to get Software Collection information of most items. Please add column 'Software Collection' to the list if it is not in the default header list.

entry.title: SAP CodeJam at Hicron (Wroclaw, Poland)
entry.content:
Hi,

we would like to organize an SAP JamCode at Hicron (Wroclaw, Poland) in the first week of July. 

Topic: Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model, SAP AI Core and SAP generative AI Hub. 

Best regards

Agnieszka

entry.title: Re: FREE_SELECTIONS_DIALOG with Field names
entry.content:
Thanks I have a small step foreward.

I modified gs_field_texts-text in the select, before FREE_SELECTIONS_INIT.
I checked it in debug mode, and it was okay, but in display it stayed default in the red (tree) area, and changed only in the slect options section marked with yellow. ☹️

CONCATENATE gs_field_texts-text '(' gs_field_texts-fieldname ')' INTO gs_field_texts-text.
APPEND gs_field_texts TO gt_field_texts.

entry.title: Re: Is the Cloud Connector feature available for Kafka Sy...
entry.content:
The Kafka adapter on CPI cannot use Cloud Connector to connect to on-premises Kafka topics: 3146484 - Does Kafka adapter on HCI/CPI support Cloud Connector?

As a workaround, you could use Edge Integration Cell, which is installed on-premises.

entry.title: Re: Can't instantiate a fragment after Destory it
entry.content: Despite following your suggestion to destroy the fragment, I'm still facing a unique ID error. I even made sure to destroy all dependents by iterating through V.getDependents() and calling oD.destroy() on each before V.destroy(). While V.getDependents() appears empty after this process, suggesting the destroy function is working, SAP seems to be holding onto these IDs in memory.

entry.title: How to display the fields vertically
entry.content:
Hi there! please how can I show the fields vertically in one column under title "Description"

 

FORM f_prepare_AFFECT_DATA2.

CALL METHOD obj_test->prepare_affect_resultat2
  EXPORTING
    it_grp_nature    = gt_grp_nature
    it_description_a =  gt_affect_a2                 
  IMPORTING
    et_affect2_alv   =  gt_alv_affect2               
  .

ENDFORM.

FORM show_affect2.

  DATA: lt_fieldcat TYPE lvc_t_fcat,
        ls_fieldcat TYPE lvc_s_fcat,
        ls_layout   TYPE lvc_s_layo.

  g_subscreen = '0107'.

  " Define Field Catalog
  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'REPORT_NOUVEAU'.
  ls_fieldcat-coltext   = 'Report Nouveau'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'RESULTAT_NET_I'.
  ls_fieldcat-coltext   = 'Résultat Net I'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'RESULTAT_NET_E'.
  ls_fieldcat-coltext   = 'Résultat Net E'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'PRELEVEMENT'.
  ls_fieldcat-coltext   = 'Prélèvement'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

  CLEAR ls_fieldcat.
  ls_fieldcat-fieldname = 'AUTRE_PRELEVEMENT'.
  ls_fieldcat-coltext   = 'Autre Prélèvement'.
  APPEND ls_fieldcat TO lt_fieldcat.

  ls_layout-zebra = abap_true.

  " Container and Grid Initialization
  IF go_container IS NOT BOUND.

    CREATE OBJECT go_container
      EXPORTING
        container_name              = 'CC_ALV1'
      EXCEPTIONS
        cntl_error                  = 1
        cntl_system_error           = 2
        create_error                = 3
        lifetime_error              = 4
        lifetime_dynpro_dynpro_link = 5
        OTHERS                      = 6.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
                 WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.
  ENDIF.

  CHECK go_container IS BOUND.

  IF go_grid IS NOT BOUND.

    CREATE OBJECT go_grid
      EXPORTING
        i_parent = go_container
      EXCEPTIONS
        error_cntl_create = 1
        error_cntl_init   = 2
        error_cntl_link   = 3
        error_dp_create   = 4
        OTHERS            = 5.
    IF sy-subrc <> 0.
      MESSAGE ID sy-msgid TYPE sy-msgty NUMBER sy-msgno
                 WITH sy-msgv1 sy-msgv2 sy-msgv3 sy-msgv4.
    ENDIF.

  ENDIF.

  CALL METHOD go_grid->set_table_for_first_display
    EXPORTING
      is_layout       = ls_layout
    CHANGING
      it_outtab       = gt_alv_affect2
      it_fieldcatalog = lt_fieldcat.

ENDFORM.

Normally it show like this:

But I want to show it like this:

Thank you

entry.title: Promotion Functionality in SuccessFactors Compensation
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have enabled promotion functionality in SuccessFactors Compensation by following this blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blog-posts-by-members/employee-central-compensation-promotion-integration-in-successfactors/ba-p/13327323

I have few queries regarding promotion functionality:

  • can we have different publishing date for Job Information publishing based on country or location (Screenshot attached)
  • Is there any report available which can provide list of employees who got promoted
  • Supposed a different budget is required for promotion then how this can be handled
  • Can we configured field like adjustment and name it as promotion to enter values only for promotion increase.
  • Best practice is to create a new position for promotion.  If that employee is having direct reportees then his direct reportees position has to be updated with new position data before doing promotion ?

Can you please provide your inputs on the same

@xavierlegarrec 

Regards,

Satish Naik

entry.title: ABAP Error
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I am configuring on the ECC EHP8 (Trial Environment), on CAL. I get the following errors when I attempt to create a purchase order and also trying to create a business partner. Please see the attached errors and assist.

entry.title: ME21N Confirmation Control Key
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have a problem. I try to new standart PO with ME21N tcode but confirmation control key field comes "0004 ınbound delivery" as a automatically, I don't want this situation. I checked the below steps but I don't reach the result.
1-) SPRO --- Logistics Execution --- Shipping --- Deliveries --- Define Order Confirmations for Inbound Deliveries (There isn't customizing with order type (purchasing)).

2-) I checked vendor  in FLVN01 Purchasing confirmation control key field with BP t-code but again confirmation control key field comes "0004 ınbound delivery" as a automatically.

How can I solve this problem?

Thx.

entry.title: Re: Unable to update it0584 subty 0002
entry.content:
I am facing the same issue too. Experts please help

Thanks

entry.title: Public Checkbox Not Visible in sap.ui.comp.smartvariants SmartVariantManagement on showShare='true'
entry.content:
I'm developing a freestyle SAPUI5 application which has FilterBar with table. I am using the demoKit sample code for this: https://ui5.sap.com/#/entity/sap.ui.comp.filterbar.FilterBar/sample/sap.ui.comp.sample.filterbar.DynamicPageListReport and  smartVariantMangement control with the following configuration:

<f:heading>
                    <svm:SmartVariantManagement id="svm" showShare="true" standardItemText="{i18n>reviewTitle}" />
                </f:heading>

Also, in my manifest.json, I have: "flexEnabled": true 

  • SAPUI5 version: 1.120.6

  • Backend: Java-based CAP application

  • Deployment: Both frontend and backend are deployed to SAP BTP (Fiori Launchpad site)

However, when I try to save a variant/view, the "Public" checkbox is not visible in the Save As or Manage Views dialogs.

Possible Issue:

I suspect this might be due to missing authorization, but I am unsure how this applies to BTP-only apps
Is there a way to enable public variant support or check missing authorizations in the BTP context?

 SAPUI5

entry.title: Re: SME files upload in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for the reply!
I have checked the 1M1 scope guide, which requires the SAP Concur Professional Edition to integrate with SAP Concur. Another document using the app does not state the edition requirement for the integration.
I would like to further know that if the Concur edition is Standard Edition, is it still possible to perform the integration the same way using the Concur Integration Setup App?

Also, If we have a three system landscape in public cloud, but only one concur system, we would want to perform testing after integration. Is it possible to change connections after we build a connection between the testing system and concur system to the production system?

Thanks!

Best Regards,
Will

entry.title: Re: Recruiting Marketing, Radial search without exposing...
entry.content: Posted the issue as an improvement request: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/349656

entry.title: Re: Split batch, Picking and PGI using single IDOC (SHPCO...
entry.content:
Hello, did you find an answer ?
I also would like to perform batch split and PGI in same idoc but did not succeed so far.

entry.title: Re: Getting error while adding New system in VS CODE using SAP Fiori generator.
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you for the details and screenshots, and sorry you are encountering this error. I'd like to ask for some further details around the current set up. 

- Can you please confirm the version of the Fiori generator you are currently using? You can check easily using the information icon in the top left corner in the UI of the Fiori Application Generator to confirm the version.

- Please check in the SAP Systems Panel, and run Test Connection against the system you are trying to use. Please refer to the Help documentation on Managing Systems Connection. Screenshots will be useful in this case. 

Best regards,

Cian 

Fiori tools team

entry.title: Alerts and notifications have been trigger even though the effective date is way passed
entry.content:
Hello team,

Let me explain the situation:

The employees were imported to production System in February 2024

notification configuration was passed in June 2024 

so we triggered a job to collect those employees in the manage alert and notifications ec alerts so they would have their  FUTURE notifications pending to be triggered.

then we triggered EC alerts and not. job selecting January 2024 as the date.

What happened to our surprise is that notifications where the THEN statement in the rule is date plus + 4 days have been triggered also, although that date is way passed, the notification has been triggered anyway.

Is this the standard behavior to launch notifications long after the effective date has already passed?

entry.title: Re: How to display the fields vertically
entry.content:
Hi,

    I think its not possible to display the fields in vertical lining as fieldcat shows the fields one after the other but not one under the other. 

Rgds,

G. Khadeer Basha

entry.title: Re: How to distribute Commodity Codes (Table /SAPSLL/MARITC) from S/4 to EEC (R/3) by IDoc?
entry.content: Hi Günter, We are on the S4 journey and we are facing similar issue. Curious to know how this issue was solved ?

entry.title: Help to close RE2 inbound delivery note
entry.content: We would like to delete a delivery note 8239001845 in Quality system . It’s got some kind-of corruption on it (maybe due to some part of the flow got archived) so they can’t delete in VL02N. we are getting short dump MESSAGE_TYPE_X in program CL_MSR_DEF_DB=================CP.

entry.title: Create new DME format under Format Tree UMS1 - S4Hana Public Cloud Edition
entry.content:
We have a requirement for the XML output from the Fiori app 'Advance Return for Tax on Sales/Purchases'. As I understand, only the Tree type UMS1 can be used. 

How can I create a new Format tree under UMS1? In the Legacy system, it was very straight forward as you only needed transaction code DMEE. I do not seem to find anything similar in the Public Cloud Edition. 

Please let me know the steps, or if it is not possible, and we should open an SAP note.

I have attached some relevant screenshots from the mentioned Fiori app.

Regards, and thank you.

entry.title: SAP HCM: Org. Unit cannot be assigned to Position (PA30)
entry.content:
I have created some new Org. Units and accordingly made relations:

1. Position to Person

2. Position to Org. Unit

BUT only Position / Person relation is visible at PA30 >>> Org. Assignment infotype. However, Org Unit is showing 00000000.

Regards,

Jamil

entry.title: SAP Project Implementation Template
entry.content:
Hello guys,

Please, i need a SAP Ariba: Procurement Project Implementation Template

entry.title: SAP Build Process Automation - How to transition to Shared Environment - Shared to all users
entry.content:
Dear Community,

We recently added a new option that allows to share an environment to all users. This would ease the transition from public environment to shared environment as explained in a previous blog post.

How it works?

You can create a shared environment as usual. Now in the sharing you have a new option:

After selecting this option, you can decide the rights applicable for all users:

The bare minimum right would be "Monitor". With "Deploy" a Process Automation Developer would be granted to deploy a project in this shared environment.

At the end you will have all the benefits from Shared Environment already mentioned here or here. It's also possible to update an existing shared environment with this option too.

Development Tenant VS. Production Tenant

  • In a "development tenant", you may enable this new shared environment with all rights... except administrate.
  • In a "production tenant", you may restrict this new shared environment with the right "execute" only.

Choice is yours as Process Automation Administrator.

More information about:

  • API Keys - help topic
  • Processes and Workflows within Environment - help topic
  • Manage Destinations - help topic
  • Triggers - help topic
  • Add an API trigger to a project - help topic
  • SAP Note for public environment sunset - https://me.sap.com/notes/3522611

entry.title: Re: Connection issue in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello @KarthikKumar31 

I think I saw this error message in the past as well... but long time ago, 1-2 years.

First thing I would do is to check if the Cloud connector can reach your SAP Test system ... means does the icon in the CC appear in green, showing the system is reached.

If not, this is the root cause.

If yes, maybe you check SU53 for your connection user, after testing it again? Can you find any entries there?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Request for Solution: CDS View with Parameters (Key D...
entry.content:
Hello @sridhar123sap 

I'm sorry, but I cannot see the list of CDS views here...

However, if the data changes permanently and the result is controlled by the key date, I would recommend to connect the CDS View directly to SAC as live model and build a story based on this.

If the CDS View is no analytic query, yet (annotation: @Analytics.query:true), then you could create one on top and use this for your live model in SAC.

In this way you can avoid loading daily data/delta and just report live on S4.

Otherwise you can maybe explain what you would like to do with the data in SAP datasphere.

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA BP screen enhancement with custom tab
entry.content:
Hi Expert.

I am using BDT to enhance BP with three more customized tab page. All functions works fine.  there is a problem. the steps are as follows:

  1. opening BP 
  2. open one of the customized tab
  3. do some changes and save.

Update log information: like updated user and date should be only save for current active tab, however, the value of the similar fields on other two tabs updated as well in database.

All fields are appended to LFA1. is there any way to deal with such situation. In ECC, three different screens can be processed individually and no side effects between them. Thanks a lot.

 

Best Regards,

Walter

entry.title: Re: Don't show null values in the chart - SAC
entry.content:
Hello @daianealves 
I think we need a bit more information here ... is it based on an import model or live model?

Have you tried setting a background-filter to prevent those lines to be shown? Where do the null values come from?

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Links to SAP demo recordings for HCM Business AI features
entry.content: Great post!

entry.title: Re: Integration flows do not work in the Integration Suit...
entry.content: Thank you

entry.title: Re: Help Portal: We're sorry, but this content is not acc...

Hi,

For access, send a message to: SAP_Private_Cloud_Help_Portal_Admin@sap.com - include your customer ID, email address and/or S-user.

This instruction appears on the following page: https://help.sap.com/docs/RISE_WITH_SAP_PRIVATE_CLOUD_EDITION

Hope this helps and best regards
Sue

entry.title: Re: Requirement to Include "From Date" and "To Date" in T...
entry.content:
Hi @hk-123 ,

I'm afraid this is not possible. However, the date range does appear on TB when exported to PDF. If you manage to export PDF > XLS, it could be a temporary measure until SAP includes the desired fields in the report output.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Item groups running with different valuation method S...
entry.content: If we cannot make the changes then what is impact or it is safe to move from Moving average to FIFO for all items groups (same as per company settings : FIFO)

entry.title: How do I know if SAP Cash Management is activated?
entry.content:
Hello!

How can we check in S/4HANA Public Cloud if SAP Cash Management is activated or not? What scope items belong to Cash Management?

entry.title: Re: 无鸟用的SAP PA证书，刚入行的同行可以考一考
entry.content: PA证书对于想系统学习SAP的人是非常有用的，作为顾问如果不完全了解SAP的模块功能就没办法给客户推荐最佳的方案，找工作方面其实SAP PA对于自由顾问来说确实没什么用，因为自由顾问要的是实战能力，而PA这种证书不能体现你的能力。但是入职Global甲方或者SAP这个证书还是有用的。

entry.title: Re: Fiori Custom Fields and Logic app error
entry.content:
Hi,

thanks for your comment.

I checked the in the transaction SOBJ the objects which are related to CUSY and I can confirm that all are set to display / change and they have a valid customizing transport request. 

I tried to look up a help in your mentioned webside but I did found one which could help me in this matter,  could you please post a direct link? 

Regards,
Adam

entry.title: Re: How to do background scheduling with clean core, including Fiori apps, replacing se38 reports
entry.content:
Hi @Juwin 

An action that allows DB insert I don't see coming, seeing as direct DB access in ABAP Cloud is not allowed but I'm also unsure about an action that allows your own commits, since the RAP framework usually takes care of it. I am not one of the makers of RAP, so can't comment on that, if that is possible.

But as for RAP being "counter-intuitive", I kindly disagree and try to explain, if you allow 😊. RAP is different, that's why it may seem counter-intuitive at first - as any language is, at first. These "restrictions"  in RAP are not restrictions, but stability features. RAP ensures that the end-to-end process of whichever behavior is to happen with your object, will be consistent and complete. Can you do a direct DB insert into a z-table in a user exit in classic ABAP? Yes you can. Is that a good thing/stable? No, not at all. You are in the middle of a safe sequence, of something that could, theoretically, still fail to save. But then you already did you direct DB update/insert, thus your data is now inconsistent with what in the end happened with your object. RAP won't allow direct DB inserts or commits during the safe sequence for exactly that reason, and that should never change.

Personally I don't think "additional save" causes any extra hassle. Does it really matter if your code runs directly in an action, unmanaged save, or where-ever you need to have it, or if it is in the additional save? In fact, having additional safe means that RAP isn't lacking in functionality. It simply forces a safe order of things. In this example here, yes it would have been nice to have the APJ creation directly in the action, I totally agree, and I even said "unfortunately we can't" above. But that isn't "RAP's fault": it's keeping my behavior safe and consistent (making sure the behavior first completes, then we can add other updates). In this case, what is missing is that APJ offers an API where I can create an application job that executes with the next commit (or at least let me decide whether I want to force commit or not) and does not always do a direct DB insert.

RAP is really powerful if you get to know it and offers so much out of the box, such as full save and read sequences with full OData query capabilities, including draft handling etc.; you don't need to code any of that. At the same time you can choose to create your own custom query provider. You can choose to create your own unmanaged save. RAP offers both ways; complete framework taking care of the main things or you do your custom codes yourself.

Hope that gave some ideas or insights.

Kind regards

entry.title: Re: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2
entry.content: Nice work. I really like the new changes, they bring clarity!

entry.title: Re: Issue with intent recognition in the Chatbot
entry.content: I'm experiencing the same issue as well. Has anyone found a solution yet?

entry.title: Re: CPI iFlow getting error: The metadata constraints '[P...
entry.content:
Hello,

the issue was that SAP changed the metadata (from Odata service API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV, old doesn't have precision=0) used in CPI iFlow. When we reactivated the iFlow, the metadata updated, and it stopped working. We had to rework all the code (based on new metadata) in our system that sends post requests to iFlow.

Regards,

Patrik

entry.title: Re: Ensure License Compliance
entry.content:
Hi @phoude,

I think I'm a little bit lost here... A Consumption node was available in Explorer Tree, same with Show Report action for Order, but neither of them did anything. Once you ran Reset Everything, both disappeared?

Would you be able to login to hac and make sure ordermetricsbackoffice extension is properly loaded?

Also - could you please use Orchestrator Mode and see in config.xml, if you have such snippet there:

    <context component="explorer-tree" authority="consumption" merge-by="authority">
        <et:explorer-tree>
            <et:navigation-node id="consumption-validation" position="350"/>
        </et:explorer-tree>
    </context>

 Also: bear in mind that you need to update system with an option to apply essential data for authority to be added.

Cheers,

Jacek

entry.title: Re: Two OPEN SQl statements with same WHERE condition, di...
entry.content: Hi @BearbelWinkler, As per your abservation, in the second SELECT statement's field list, I included the key fields from the PLPO table: plpo~plnty, plpo~plnnr, and plpo~plnkn. This ensured that I received the same output. Thank you very much for sharing your knowledge!

entry.title: Technical Error in PCC
entry.content:
Hi,

If you use SAP S/4HANA Cloud, please create an incident. Otherwise, if \"Runtime Error\" is not initial, launch ADT feed \"Runtime Errors\" or transaction ST22 / SM21 (system log analysis) for details and analysis." Can anyone suggest how to resolve the issue.

Thank you

Swapna

entry.title: Re: Your First Step into AI with SAP: Start Learning, Get Certified, Drive Innovation

Hello Arabella,

Thank you for above information. I was wondering how to practice for this course since I have SAP BTP Trial Account. Please Advice. Thanks.

entry.title: Are ABAP Developers Ready for AI with SAP Joule?
entry.content:
I have been actively involved in the SAP ERP domain for nearly 30 years and have witnessed numerous pivotal innovations across SAP’s evolving technology landscape. My ABAP journey began in 1996, when I developed custom code for SAP R/2 HR. As a consultant, I’ve experienced the complete evolution of ABAP—from its early days to the most recent releases—closely following every significant change, enhancement, and breakthrough. Over the past decade, these innovations have had a profound impact on the developer community. However, in my view, no shift has been as revolutionary as the introduction of Joule for Developers and the rise of AI capabilities within the ABAP environment. In this blog, I will share my thoughts with you.

In October 2024, I had the privilege of participating in SAP’s internal workshop series, Joule’s ABAP Developer Capabilities, through my role as an SAP Mentor. This engagement provided early access to Joule for Developers within ABAP in Eclipse, enabling me to conduct hands-on testing, provide structured evaluations, and contribute feedback ahead of its official launch in February 2025. I use ChatGPT regularly both at work and in my personal life. However, during these workshops, I realized my lack of in-depth knowledge about AI.

I realized that to be future-proof, I must invest in myself, and I was happy that my company entrusted me with developing an AI introduction training program tailored for SAP developers. Additionally, in one of my client engagements, my responsibilities as a solution architect were expanded to include the role of AI advisor. In this role, I had the opportunity to join a dedicated two-week hackathon, where I explored how existing AI capabilities could be leveraged effectively and identified key success factors for deploying AI within complex SAP landscapes. With these opportunities, I'm on my way to closing the AI knowledge gap necessary for success as an SAP Architect and SAP developer, and I want to share some of my experiences.

Joule for Developers

As an SAP enthusiast, I began my journey at the SAP Learning Hub to expand my knowledge and skills. And even though Joule for Developers is relatively new, I found detailed information in the delta course to be certified as an ABAP developer. In this course, it is explained that Joule for Developers supports SAP ABAP developers with AI-based capabilities throughout the development process. This includes scenarios like

  • Predictive code completion: generating ABAP code in the source code editor
  • Joule chat: natural language interaction with an AI-based chat to get support for any development-related questions
  • Explain: explaining development objects or selected lines of code in natural language
  • ABAP Unit: generating and improving unit tests
  • CDS test generation: creating ABAP test classes for CDS entities

The Code Assistant is integrated into the ABAP development tools in Eclipse. It functions as a chatbot that understands complex development objects within ABAP Cloud, allowing developers to ask interactive, natural-language questions and provides real-time answers.

And with the ABAP AI SDK in ABAP Cloud, the developer has even the possible to develop custom generative AI features directly within your business applications. But this capability is currently (June 2025) only possible when your application is running on an ABAP Cloud environment (such as SAP BTP ABAP Environment or ABAP Cloud in SAP S/4HANA Cloud). You need also access to SAP AI Core and Generative AI Hub, which you only get when your company has an SAP BTP subscription for SAP AI Core, SAP AI Launchpad, and Generative AI Hub.

For many developers, the journey with Joule for Developers has remained out of reach due to high costs of the BTP services and limited availability within their company SAP environments—but that’s about to change. With the September 2025 release of S/4HANA, AI capabilities will become available for both on-premise and private cloud deployments. SAP also plans to extend compatibility to the 2023 S/4HANA releases. This means we’re just a few months away from a major opportunity with Joule and the broader AI potential SAP is unlocking for developers.

However, as Joule becomes accessible to a wider group of developers, this shift also raises important questions:

Are ABAP developers prepared to leverage the AI capabilities of SAP Joule for Developers entirely?
Are we, as a developer community, truly ready for this transformation?
If not, should we not seek additional guidance and support to navigate this new chapter successfully?

These are critical questions because the answers will ultimately determine the success of Joule for Developers. To explore the answers to these questions, I decided to dive into hands-on research and practical testing.

Research and practical testing

During the development of the AI training, I frequently used ChatGPT to find answers to my questions. Because I also had access to Joule for Developers on an embedded cloud environment of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition, I could check my thoughts and findings directly in Joule for Developers.

I began by asking some general questions about Joule, directing them to both ChatGPT and the Joule platform. Interestingly, both AI tools provided similar responses. SAP Joule is specifically tailored to understand ABAP, SAP data models, and business context, making it more suitable for SAP-centric development. In contrast, tools like OpenAI's ChatGPT and GitHub Copilot offer more general-purpose coding assistance across a wide range of programming languages and platforms.

All the AI tools recommended using SAP Joule as an assistant for ABAP development. However, as a seasoned ABAP developer—with a few gray hairs but an ABAP Cloud certificate—I need more than just a recommendation. I want to be convinced. So, I decided to put both Joule and ChatGPT to the test by asking them to create a function module using the following prompt:

Prompting for an ABAP function

Both tools provided similar ABAP code for the function module. However, Joule went a step further by also offering a step-by-step guide on how to create the function module within an SAP ERP system.

Response from Joule

However, here’s the catch: neither response enables me to run the function module in my S/4HANA Cloud, public edition system. The provided code doesn’t align with clean core principles and utilizes unreleased artifacts. Moreover, Joule’s step-by-step guide is essentially useless in this context, as I’m not allowed to use SAP GUI or traditional transaction codes in a public cloud environment.

 

As an ABAP developer—especially one without a background in AI—I found this experience frustrating. From an SAP-specific AI assistant, I expect valid ABAP code tailored to my system landscape, along with a step-by-step guide that reflects the actual constraints and best practices of S/4HANA Cloud.

Naturally, I tried to solve the issue by asking follow-up questions, but I continued to struggle with incomplete or incorrect answers. It wasn’t until I deepened my understanding of generative AI, large language models (LLMs), and prompt engineering that I began to see why the AI tools responded the way they did. In the world of AI, these issues are referred to as bias and hallucinations. In other words, the problem wasn’t an error in the AI tools themselves—it stemmed from my initial lack of understanding.

It also reminds me of a key insight I came across while researching for the training I developed. In the paper 'On the Dangers of Stochastic Parrots: Can Language Models Be Too Big?' by Emily M. Bender, Timnit Gebru, Angelina McMillan-Major, and Margaret Mitchell, the authors emphasize — in my own words

GPTs are trained to generate human-like text, not necessarily accurate text.

I found this to be especially true, notably when someone lacks a solid understanding of how generative AI and large language models work. And this so true for the question I asked.

Both models were trained on vast amounts of ABAP code and SAP documentation, learning to identify and reproduce common patterns. So, while Joule was designed to generate ABAP code and step-by-step guides, it wasn't necessarily generating correct or system-appropriate solutions. It was doing exactly what it was trained to do: generating content that looks plausible but is not guaranteed to be valid in a specific context, such as S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.

Prompt Engineering

Several techniques—collectively known as AI grounding—can be applied to overcome incorrect answers caused by bias and hallucinations. Some of these, such as supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF), are the responsibility of the AI vendor—in this case, SAP, the provider of the Joule assistant. SAP has already made considerable progress in applying these methods to improve the quality and reliability of Joule's responses.

However, the user of AI tools often has the most significant influence on the accuracy of AI-generated answers—in our case, the ABAP developer. By using effective prompt techniques, selecting the proper AI tool settings, and adhering to established best practices for prompting, developers can significantly enhance the quality and relevance of the answers they receive. Prompting well has become a dedicated discipline, leading to the creation of a new job: the Prompt Engineer. This means that the ABAP developer should learn a new skill, prompt engineering, at least to become a successful developer with SAP Joule.

If I try the same prompt question as before, add context, and follow the zero-shot chain-of-thought (COT) task step-by-step, a widely used reasoning technique in prompt engineering, the Joule result improves significantly.

Zero-shot COT promptResponse from Joule

In the response, Joule provides a step-by-step guide that aligns well with my ABAP development environment in Eclipse. It clearly outlines what I need to do and suggests creating a class rather than using a function module—an approach more typical in an ABAP Cloud environment. It also highlights that direct access to SAP tables and unreleased APIs is not permitted, which aligns with ABAP's core principles of cleanliness.

The provided code skeleton matches what I would expect in an ABAP Cloud setup. However, it falls short in one crucial aspect: it doesn't specify the correct ABAP CDS view to use.

As an ABAP developer, I’d naturally ask follow-up questions like:

What is the CDS view for customer information?

Which released CDS views are available for customers?

Response from Joule

But the answers are often unhelpful, forcing me to search and experiment on my own, which hurts productivity.

A skilled prompt engineer, however, would recognize this approach as weak. Instead, they would refine the prompt using additional context and prompt engineering techniques, iterating multiple times until the correct result is achieved. He will likely develop a prompt like this. In many cases, these tailored prompts can also serve as reusable templates for similar scenarios in the future.

Optimized prompt

Response of Joule

This response reflects the level of support I expect from a SAP AI assistant—providing clear guidance and ready-to-run code.

Conclusion

Based on my research, I can conclude that ABAP developers need at least basic prompt engineering skills—and ideally, a broader understanding of general AI concepts—to effectively utilize SAP Joule for Developers and fully leverage its AI capabilities. Currently, most ABAP developers lack these skills, which limits their ability to derive the maximum value from the tool.

In my opinion, the developer community is not yet fully prepared for the AI transformation, primarily due to a lack of foundational knowledge in AI. I was fortunate to be in a position that allowed me to conduct this research and enhance my understanding of AI while developing AI training for our company. However, this is not the case for most ABAP developers.

To ensure success, SAP needs to provide more structured guidance and support. At the same time, companies must invest in upskilling their developers to capitalize on the opportunities offered by SAP Joule fully.

This article was initially posted on the linked-in profile of Robert Eijpe 

Disclaimer:
SAP Joule is an AI tool, and the results of prompts may vary over time or when repeated. Thus, the responses Joule provides can differ from those shown in this blog.

entry.title: Re: I have a business requirement in SAP EWM
entry.content: Hi Ajit

entry.title: Re: Work Around for Org Replication error –Manager is not valid in the Period
entry.content:
@ramya_26 you probably found the solution already, but you can search in SPRO "Restrict Employee Master Data Replication to Certain Countries" (under "Integration with SuccessFactors Employee Central" => "Other Integration Settings" => "Replicating Data from Employee Central to SAP ERP" => "Country Filtering") there you list all the countries that are in scope. So let's say, if you have Germany, but not Austria, the system will not try to replicate or assign an employee to an Austrian manager. 

Obviously if you have a structure with mixed countries, this will create "holes" in your SAP OM structure. This can be an issue if you have workflow or approval processes on S/4 side.

entry.title: Configuration of certificates and e-mails for Milestone Awards
entry.content:
Milestones Awards work slightly different comparing to Spot Awards:

  • There is no certificate created under Rewards & Recognitions tab
  • There are no e-mails triggered to a nominee/his or her manager.

This was frequently the reason, why my customer didn´t want to proceed with this piece of Comp&Ben. For one customer we had to create a workaround to meet above stated requirements. In this blog I would like to share tips and tricks so you can save the time re-inventing the wheel.

Step 1 – Create a document generation group with a category "Workflow".

This is essential to be able to send an e-mail.

 

Step 2 – Define Document template.

Here we will configure the layout of an e-mail notification or a certificate. Unfortunately, the sent out e-mail can´t be saved in the system.

The first image is showing the certificate template which will be sent as an e-mail. This can be configured via the UI editor as well as directly in HTML.

The second image is showing e-mail template.

Step 3 – Once the template has been created and linked to a group, we need to map placeholders to respective data.

The challenges we’ve encountered here were related to the name of an employee and number of years. Number of years in our case determine when employees receive a milestone award. This data cannot be taken as a reference data from the program itself and should come from a business rule.

Step 4 – Create respective business rules.

Step 5 – Define a workflow to trigger your e-mails

Step 6 – Assign this workflow to a business rule, which will be then mapped to a non-recurring pay component.

Step 7 – Map your general business rule to non-recurring pay component

To test the proposed solution above, please keep in mind the following limitations:

  • Test users should have a unique e-mail. As soon as the system encounters that e-mail address repeats, no e-mail will be sent out.
  • There is a possibility to trigger respective eligibility job for milestone awards only once a day manually, so prepare your test data carefully before you trigger the scheduled job.
  • The scheduled job itself is running once a day.
  • There is no way to show to manager milestone awards of his/her team under Awards History.

If you have any adjustments or enhancement to this article, please write them as a comment.

 

Kind regards,

Anna

entry.title: Re: How do I know if SAP Cash Management is activated?
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, you can check whether SAP Cash Management is activated and see which scope items are related to it by following these steps:

 

How to check if SAP Cash Management is activated :

1. Go to “Manage Your Solution” App (or “Configure Your Solution” for implementation projects):

    • Navigate to App Finder > search for “Manage Your Solution”.
    • Select the “Set Up Your Solution” tile.

 

2. Search for Scope Items:

    • In the Implementation Activities view, click on the “Scope” tab.
    • Enter “Cash Management” or specific scope item IDs in the search field.
    • Look for relevant scope items (see list below) and check their status (e.g., “Activated”).

 

3. Check the Configuration Environment:

    • In the “Manage Your Solution” app, under Application Area: Finance > Sub Application Area: Cash and Liquidity Management, check if related configuration steps are available.
    • If they are grayed out, Cash Management may not be activated.

 

4. Use Apps Related to Cash Management:

  • Cash Position Details
  • Liquidity Forecast
  • Manage Bank Accounts
    • Try opening apps like:
    • If these are unavailable or throw errors, it likely means the scope is not active.

Scope items related to SAP Cash Management

Here are the main scope items associated with SAP Cash Management in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition:

Scope Item

Name

Description

1SG

Cash Management

Core cash management setup including cash position and liquidity forecast

J78

Bank Relationship Management

Master data for bank accounts, signatories, overdraft limits

BFA

Advanced Cash Operations

Includes grouping, pooling, bank fees, and intercompany netting

1KV

Liquidity Planning with SAP Analytics Cloud

Integrates cash planning with SAC

22C

Intercompany Netting

Facilitates automated netting of payables and receivables across group companies

3FF

In-House Cash

For large enterprises managing centralized payments and internal bank accounts

1J2

Integration with Bank Communication Management (BCM)

Payment control and approval workflows

 

I hope those elements will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Session 1 Recap & Highlights– Data Inspired – Successful concepts for data-driven organizations
entry.content:
Our first speaker Helmut Grimm talked about the concept of Flywheel that was introduced as part of Christian Klein’s keynote at SAP Sapphire 2025. Grimm introduced SAP's transformative flywheel concept, positioning data as the critical center of organizational innovation. He emphasized that in the age of AI, the interplay between applications, data, and artificial intelligence has become more crucial than ever. The flywheel metaphor illustrates how data acts as the fundamental engine driving business acceleration, requiring a seamless integration of technological components.

 Helmut emphasized that today,  Data sits at the center of organizational innovation and best of suite approach enables seamless interplay between apps, data, and AI that is extremely critical. Helmut also introduced the newly launched SAP Architecture Center as a strategic resource for data integration and must visit for every EA.

Our next speaker, Dr. Sebastian Wernicke laid the vision of going from Data-Driven to Data-Inspired. Sebastian challenged traditional data utilization approaches, introducing the revolutionary concept of being "data-inspired" rather than merely "data-driven". He argued that most organizations struggle not with data collection, but with transforming data insights into meaningful organizational change.

These challenges include Persistent skills gaps in driving value from data, Unclear business cases for data initiatives, insufficient management attention to data strategies, difficulty in breaking down organizational silos and Limited ability to transform data insights into actionable change within and outside the organization.

 

While there are data challenges in every org, it is an great opportunity for Data Architects and Enterprise Architects to change things by driving – holistic organizational learning and through continuous conversation with senior executives about their data initiatives.

Sebastian also talk about how Enterprise Architects can drive towards being Data Inspired through these 4 element that are usually in their sphere of influence:

  1. Radical Data Integrity -  The foundational approach to building trustworthy data ecosystems. Enterprise architects must develop robust governance frameworks that ensure data quality, create transparent lineage, and implement metadata management strategies. This involves not just technological solutions, but also developing organizational cultures that prioritize data accuracy and reliability.
  2. Connected Data Ecosystems bringing a holistic approach to breaking down traditional organizational silos. Enterprise architects must design integrated platforms that facilitate seamless data sharing across different functional areas. This involves creating technical infrastructures and collaborative mechanisms that encourage cross-functional communication and insight generation.
  3. Strategic Incentive Structures i.e. reimagining of organizational reward systems. By aligning incentives with data-sharing and insight generation, enterprise architects can create cultural mechanisms that inherently value data-driven decision-making. This goes beyond traditional performance metrics, embedding a data-inspired mindset into the core of organizational behavior.
  4. Decision Architecture Design as a critical capability for modern enterprise architects. This involves developing flexible frameworks that integrate data insights directly into strategic decision-making processes. The goal is to create organizational systems that support continuous learning, adaptability, and strategic responsiveness.

 The inaugural webinar of the series was a huge success with the participants as the speakers presented a compelling narrative that goes far beyond traditional data management, positioning data as a strategic asset capable of driving fundamental organizational change. As SAP customers start their Business Data Cloud journey, the speakers acknowledged that it is not about accumulating more data or implementing sophisticated technologies, but about fundamentally reimagining how organizations perceive, utilize, and derive value from their data ecosystems. Enterprise architects stand at the forefront of this transformation, serving as critical architects of not just technological infrastructure, but of organizational change and adaptability.

You can check out Sebastian's book here: https://www.amazon.com/Data-inspired-author/dp/3800671298

Note: Slides from the session and the recording would be only available to the registered attendees. So if you have not registered, please register now using the link below: https://go4.events.sap.com/shift-eaa-bdc-webinar/en_us/home.html

Important links:

  1. Architecture Center: https://architecture.learning.sap.com/
  2. Enterprise Architect Learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-enterprise-architecture
  3. SAP Business Data Cloud Learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/courses/introducing-sap-business-data-cloud

@PaulKurchina @moshenaveh @ralph_richter @JaSoN_Luo 

SAP Business Data Cloud   SAP Datasphere   Data and Analytics   SAP Analytics Cloud   BW (SAP Business Warehouse)

entry.title: Session 2 Recap & Highlights– Unleash your Data and AI with Irfan Khan and Timo Elliott
entry.content:
The session started with Irfan doing a quick round up of SAP BDC starting with the Why? behind the launch of Business Data Cloud. Irfan pointed a fundamental problem: businesses spend approximately 82% of their time on data curation & manipulation rather than business transformation. Here, SAP Business Data Cloud emerges as a revolutionary solution, designed to flip this paradigm by providing a modern Lake House architecture that harmonizes data across SAP and non-SAP systems.

Irfan highlighted that the key breakthrough of BDC is its approach to data products. By the end of 2024, SAP plans to offer around 500 data products, each representing intricate details of various business applications. The push logic mechanism allows seamless data ingestion from core ERP systems, creating a more efficient and intelligent data management ecosystem.

During the Q&A session, Timo Elliott skillfully extracted critical insights from Khan. One pivotal discussion revolved around data quality - a perennial challenge in enterprise computing to which Irfan candidly acknowledged that while AI can help improve data matching and cleanup, organizations must still institutionalize robust data governance practices.

Irfan talked about importance of knowledge graphs - a technology that enables more nuanced, context-aware data interactions. By using vertices and edges to identify patterns and similarities, knowledge graphs can answer complex, open-ended business questions that traditional SQL interfaces cannot.  Irfan also talked about SAP BDC being an open platform designed to work with various data sources and technologies. The partnership with Databricks, for instance, demonstrates SAP's commitment to providing flexible, interoperable solutions.

 Here are some key highlights from the Q&A session between Timo Elliott and Irfan Khan:

  1. Timo: What are the key technologies in Business Data Cloud that you're most proud of?

Irfan's highlighted the foundation service built on a modern Lake House architecture, with a unique push logic for data ingestion. He was particularly excited about creating a data product hierarchy that has been open-sourced on GitHub, allowing customers and partners to build their own data products already.

  1. Timo: What makes intelligent applications different from previous analytics applications?

Irfan's emphasized 3 key differences:

  • The integration of agents as an integral part of the applications
  • Ensuring that these applications are grounded in reliable, high-quality data with preserved business context
  • The ability to create more functionally rich applications that can curate data from both SAP and non-SAP sources
  1. Timo: How do customers with existing SAP data platforms like BW or HANA transition to Business Data Cloud?

Irfan explained that  customers have multiple options:

  • They can semantically onboard their existing data products into BDC
  • For BW systems, this could mean modernizing by bringing in data and building AI/ML capabilities on top of curated data products
  • There's flexibility to run existing systems in parallel with BDC
  1. Timo: How is SAP addressing data quality, a long-standing challenge?

Irfan acknowledged that data quality remains a complex issue. While AI can help with tasks like address matching, organizations must still:

  • Institutionalize data governance
  • Use proper master data management tools
  • Ensure data quality before implementation projects
  • Leverage BDC's capabilities to provide curated, semantically enriched data

As the webcast drew to a close, the conversation between Timo Elliott and Irfan Khan crystallized a powerful message for enterprise architects: we are standing at the precipice of a transformative era in Data and Artificial Intelligence. SAP Business Data Cloud is not just a technological solution, but a strategic capability that empowers organizations to transcend traditional data limitations. By harmonizing SAP and non-SAP data, embedding intelligent agents, and providing a robust, flexible architecture, SAP is offering more than a product – it's providing a vision of how businesses can truly unlock the potential of their data. For enterprise architects, this represents an unprecedented opportunity to move from being mere technology managers to becoming strategic innovators who can reshape how organizations understand, interact with, and derive value from their most critical asset: their data. The future is not about managing data; it's about orchestrating intelligence. To all the Enterprise Architects attending the session, the message was clear: the future belongs to those who can effectively harmonize data, leverage AI, and create intelligent, context-aware systems.

Note: Slides from the session and the recording would be only available to the registered attendees. So if you have not registered, please register now using the link below: https://go4.events.sap.com/shift-eaa-bdc-webinar/en_us/home.html

Important links:

  1. Architecture Center: https://architecture.learning.sap.com/
  2. Enterprise Architect Learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-enterprise-architecture
  3. SAP Business Data Cloud Learning Journey: https://learning.sap.com/courses/introducing-sap-business-data-cloud

@PaulKurchina @moshenaveh @ralph_richter @JaSoN_Luo @Timo 

SAP Business Data Cloud   SAP Datasphere   Data and Analytics   SAP Analytics Cloud   BW (SAP Business Warehouse)

entry.title: How to set up an ATC check run for enhancements in an S/4HANA 758 system?
entry.content:
Hi All!

After resolving the issue with getting an ATC tool failure when checking enhancements in an S/4HANA 758 system, I'm now trying to include enhancements in a check run but the provided objects are ignored and no check happens from what I can tell.

I'm including both the enhancement names in our Z-namespace as well as the enhanced SAP objects SAPMV45A and SAPMV50A but compared to a NW 750 system I can no longer explicitely include enhanced objects as the checkbox no longer exists:

Regardless of which combination of object set or free object selection I try, the check ends after a few seconds as if it's not even looking at any of the objects to check:

When I run a comparable check in our central ATC system which is on S/4HANA 756 for an enhancement of SAPMV45A in a NW 750 system, it runs a couple of minutes and produces a result with expected findings:

Does anybody know what the trick is to get a comparable result for enhancements in an S/4HANA 758 system?

This is what I provide when defining the check run, both in the central ATC-system for a check in NW 750 and the new S4HANA development system:

Thanks much and cheers

Bärbel

entry.title: Re: ME21N Confirmation Control Key
entry.content: Hi @Vijay_SAP , I want to empty this field. How can I do this?
